title: Aureline capability-inventory seed envelope
description: Boundary schema for artifacts/governance/capability_inventory_seed.yaml. The envelope pins the canonical entry vocabulary at capability_inventory_entry.schema.json, the reviewer-facing landing page, the build-identity record the validation lane embeds, the validation-lane registry ref, the closed lifecycle-state vocabulary used for M1 surfaces, the named-runtime-consumer set the seed asserts is live, the named failure-drill ids the per-row drills must draw from, the M1 surface coverage list (the capability_ids the seed must seed as M1 surface seed members), and the entry list itself. Rows conform to the entry schema; the envelope cannot relax that contract.

vocabulary.description: Optional human-readable vocabulary echo. The lane validates these against the entry schema's $defs.

lifecycle_state_vocabulary.description: Closed vocabulary of lifecycle states the seed asserts. MUST agree with the entry schema's lifecycle_state $defs and MUST cover the canonical Labs / Preview / Beta / Stable / Deprecated set.

failure_drill_id_vocabulary.description: Closed vocabulary of named failure drills the per-row failure_drill blocks must draw from.

named_runtime_consumers.description: Named live consumers that read the inventory. The lane resolves consumer_ref on disk and asserts consumed_fields is non-empty so a consumer cannot regress to 'mentioned but never read'.

required_lifecycle_state_coverage.description: M1 lifecycle states that MUST appear at least once across the entries (Labs / Preview / Beta / Stable / Deprecated).

required_m1_surface_coverage.description: capability_ids that MUST be present as m1_surface_seed_membership = true rows. Covers the protected M1 surfaces the spec calls out: shell, Start Center, workspace entry, editor basics, quick open, search shell, terminal seed, support export, and help/About.
